preamble.summary: The vision of the ram and the goat. The chapter shifts from Aramaic to Hebrew in the MT. Both Greek versions translate from a Hebrew Vorlage. The OG and Theodotion are relatively close here, as the apocalyptic imagery is preserved consistently.
preamble.notable_variants: Minor differences in the description of the little horn's actions. The OG occasionally expands the interpretation sections. The identification of the ram (Persia) and goat (Greece) is explicit in all versions.
preamble.structural_notes: All three versions have 27 verses. Chapter 8 returns to Hebrew in the MT (from Aramaic in 2:4-7:28).

The OG reads 'one strong horn' (keras hen ischyron) rather than the MT's 'a little horn' (qeren achat mits'irah). Theodotion preserves 'little.' The OG variant removes the 'smallness' motif, emphasizing power instead.

verses.variant_rendering: up to the power of heaven
verses.mt_rendering: up to the host of heaven
variant_notes: OG renders tseva as dynamis (power); Theodotion has stratia (host/army). The MT tseva can mean either.
OG uses archistratēgos (chief general), a military title. Theodotion has archōn tēs dynameōs. The figure is usually identified as God or the archangel Michael.
No significant variant between the LXX/Theodotion and the MT for this verse.

verses.mt_rendering: two thousand three hundred evenings and mornings
variant_notes: All versions agree on the number 2,300. The MT's 'evening-morning' formula is debated — does it mean 2,300 full days or 1,150 days (2,300 evening and morning sacrifices)? The Greek simply says 'days,' favoring the full-day interpretation.

verses.lxx_reading: ὁ κριός... βασιλεὺς Μήδων καὶ Περσῶν
verses.variant_rendering: the ram... king of Medes and Persians
verses.mt_rendering: the ram... the kings of Media and Persia
variant_notes: All versions explicitly identify the ram. The OG uses singular 'king'; the MT has plural 'kings.'

verses.lxx_reading: ὁ τράγος τῶν αἰγῶν — βασιλεὺς Ἑλλήνων
verses.variant_rendering: the he-goat — king of the Greeks
verses.mt_rendering: the male goat — the king of Greece
variant_notes: Both Greek versions identify Yavan (Greece/Ionia) as Hellēnōn. The great horn is Alexander.
No significant variant between the LXX/Theodotion and the MT for this verse.
